headline: Economy Won’t Grow by 4%-5% Like Predicted, Former Trump Adviser Says
summary: President Trump’s former chief economic adviser doesn't agree with the administration’s predictions that the U.S. economy will grow by 4%-5% this year.  Cohn still had a positive outlook that the economy would add jobs thanks to pockets of demand, such as cement-layers for data centers, and the productivity boosts from artificial intelligence.  Cohn also defended the H-1B visa program—a pathway for skilled workers to come to the U.S. that the Trump administration has sought to restrict—as a positive for GDP growth.

Cohn also defended the H-1B visa program—a pathway for skilled workers to come to the U.S. that the Trump administration has sought to restrict—as a positive for GDP growth.

headline: How IBM Quantum is Enabling Healthcare and Biology Research
summary: Quantum computing is at an inflection point. In recent years, quantum computers have shown the ability to run quantum programs at a scale beyond exact classical simulation. They're becoming useful tools for solving real-world problems, with provable quantum advantage close on the horizon. Community-led initiatives that provide funding and prizes for high-quality research can offer an early look at how quantum computing will impact fields like healthcare and the life sciences.

raw.headline: How IBM Quantum is Enabling Healthcare and Biology Research

headline: Ribbon-Cutting Ceremony Marks Official Opening of the Full Sail University IBM Cyber Defense Range Powered by AWS and Cloud Range
summary: Today, Full Sail University, officially opened the "Full Sail University IBM Cyber Defense Range powered by AWS and Cloud Range" following a ribbon-cutting ceremony held on the university's Winter Park, FL campus. The new state-of-the-art facility marks a significant milestone in the university's continued investment in emerging technologies education and workforce readiness.

headline: IBM and the University of Illinois Urbana-Champaign Expand Discovery Accelerator Institute to Advance AI and Quantum Computing
summary: IBM (NYSE: IBM) and the Grainger College of Engineering at the University of Illinois Urbana-Champaign (U. of I.) today announced an expansion of the IBM-Illinois Discovery Accelerator Institute. This includes deploying quantum-centric supercomputing to Illinois innovators through the integration of U. of I.'s National Center for Supercomputing Applications (NCSA) Delta and DeltaAI supercomputers with IBM quantum computers.
